docs.title: opensearch-log
docs.text:
opensearch-log is a Python logging handler for direct and efficient log transmission to OpenSearch or CloudWatch.

With context manager or function decorator you can add additional fields to log messages.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install opensearch-log

docs.title: TL;DR
docs.text:
from opensearch_log import Logging
from opensearch_log.opensearch_handler import get_logger

logger = get_logger(index_name="myindex", echo_stdout=True)
with Logging(my_log_field="From Python"):
    logger.info("Hello World")

Will send to OpenSearch something like (I cleaned up the output for readability): 

{
  "_index": "myindex-2023.12.16",
  "_source": {
    "@timestamp": "2023-12-16T06:39:19.479Z",
    "msg": "Hello World",
    "my_log_field": "From Python"
  }
}

And will print on terminal (cleaned up for readability): 

{
  "message": "Hello World",
  "name": "root",
  "my_log_field": "From Python"
}

if you need just JSON log import get_logger from opensearch_log.stdout_handler instead of opensearch_log.opensearch_handler

docs.title: Modules
docs.text:
• base_handler: Base logger for structured logging.
  • cloudwatch_handler: Structured logger that sends logs to AWS CloudWatch.
  • json_log: JSON logging.
  • opensearch_handler: Structured logger that sends logs to AWS OpenSearch.
  • opensearch_serializer: JSON serializer.
  • stdout_handler: JSON logging to stdout.

docs.title: Classes
docs.text:
• base_handler.BaseHandler: Abstract base handler for structured logging.
  • cloudwatch_handler.CloudwatchHandler: Handler that sends log records to AWS CloudWatch.
  • json_log.Logging: Context manager to add fields to log records.
  • opensearch_handler.IndexRotation: Index rotation frequency.
  • opensearch_handler.StructuredOpensearchHandler: Handler to send log records to AWS OpenSearch.
  • opensearch_serializer.OpenSearchSerializer: Override OpenSearch JSON serializer.
  • stdout_handler.StdoutHandler: Handler to send json log to stdout.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
• cloudwatch_handler.get_json_logger: Create a logger that stream logs to CloudWatch.
  • json_log.add_log_fields: Include fields to all log records.
  • json_log.create_logger: Create a logger that stream logs in JSON format with additional fields.
  • json_log.get_json_formatter: Get a JSON formatter.
  • json_log.get_logger: Get a JSON logger.
  • json_log.log_fields: Decorate to include fields values to all log records.
  • json_log.remove_handlers: Remove any default handlers.
  • json_log.remove_log_fields: Remove logged fields.
  • json_log.replace_logger_fields: Update logger with new fields to log.
  • json_log.set_record_factory: Set a log record factory.
  • opensearch_handler.get_logger: Create a logger that stream logs to OpenSearch.
  • stdout_handler.add_stdout_json_handler: Add a stdout handler to the logger.
  • stdout_handler.get_logger: Get a logger to send JSON logs to stdout.

docs.title: method flush
docs.text:
flush() → None

Flush the buffer to CloudWatch.

Could run from Timer's thread or from the main thread. So we should not use json_formatter's routines that access ThreadLocal variables.
